executive_summary: CISA has ordered federal agencies to immediately patch a maximum-severity Cisco flaw (CVE-2026-20131) and critical vulnerabilities in Oracle Identity Manager (CVE-2026-21992), Apple iOS (CVE-2025-31277, CVE-2025-43510, CVE-2025-43520), and Craft CMS (CVE-2025-32432) — all actively exploited in the wild. A massive IRS-themed phishing campaign has compromised 29,000 users, deploying remote management malware. Crunchyroll suffered a supply chain breach exposing 100GB of user data, while ShinyHunters claims to have stolen data from Ameriprise Financial involving Salesforce systems. Iranian-linked actors are using Telegram as command-and-control infrastructure to target dissidents, and Russian state hackers are conducting mass phishing attacks against high-value individuals through Signal and WhatsApp.

canada_landscape.summary: No official CCCS advisories were identified in the reporting period. Canadian entities faced multiple threats: a copyright-themed PureLog stealer campaign targeted Canadian users, Rogers (a major Canadian telecom) allegedly had customer data offered for sale on dark web markets, and insider threat concerns are rising among Canadian organizations. A dark web operator running 373,000 fraudulent sites with Canadian nexus was seized by authorities.

report_2w.executive_summary: The US Department of Justice disrupted three massive IoT botnets (AISURU, Kimwolf, JackSkid) responsible for a record-breaking 31.4 Tbps DDoS attack that impacted Canadian infrastructure. Cisco's Secure Firewall Management Center zero-day (CVE-2026-20131) is being actively exploited by Interlock ransomware 36 days before a patch was available. CISA added five new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including actively exploited flaws in Microsoft SharePoint, Apple iOS, and Zimbra Collaboration Suite. US authorities formally linked Iran's Handala hacktivist group to the Iranian government and seized their infrastructure following attacks on US healthcare provider Stryker. Over 7,500 Magento e-commerce sites were defaced in a global campaign exploiting CVE-2025-54236.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada was directly impacted by the record-breaking 31.4 Tbps DDoS attack from IoT botnets that the US DOJ disrupted this week. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period. One analytical report on strengthening cybersecurity in Canada's municipal sector was published, highlighting vulnerabilities in local government infrastructure.
